title: [Support] Add ISOP extraction utilities to TruthTable
body:
This is a preparation for SOP-balancing pass. 

This patch adds support for extracting Irredundant Sum-of-Products (ISOP) covers from truth tables using the Minato-Morreale algorithm. An ISOP is a minimal two-level representation where no cube can be removed and all cubes are pairwise disjoint.

The implementation adds Cube and SOPForm structures to represent product terms and sum-of-products expressions. The extractISOP function takes a truth table and number of variables, returning an ISOP cover computed through recursive Shannon expansion.

The algorithm is based on papers by Minato (SASIMI 1992) and Morreale (IEEE TC 1970), with implementation inspired by the lsils/kitty library. It uses a top-down variable ordering approach that partitions minterms into negative-only, positive-only, and shared cofactors to build the ISOP incrementally.
